passage.title: Helper Station Sign Project
passage.text: The group-work board team wanted children to follow team steps more clearly. At first, the group used a colorful board that mixed role names, rules, and timing notes together, but children could not tell what to do first during teamwork time. Class helpers said "The chart is colorful, yet I do not know my first team step." After hearing that, the team made the team starter job larger than the others and added one action word under every role title. They also placed one extra team card near the project tray so the extra team card repeated the next move before the project slowed down. On the next test day, children followed their teamwork order more steadily on the next practice day. The team noticed that matching table cards made the whole system easier to remember. Because of that result, the school decided to apply the new role style to other shared tasks.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Making team jobs easier for children to understand
Painting a room with brighter colors
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Students could not tell what to do next
Children could not tell what to do first
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the group add under every role title?
choices: One action word
Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does role mean in the first sentence?
choices: A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
The job a person has in a group
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
Clearer order and shorter titles reduced teamwork confusion
It made the room much larger
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Ask students to memorize everything
Apply the new role style to other shared tasks
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
